task_input: Core router CR-04 dropped all BGP sessions at 02:13; downstream OLT sites in region south lost upstream connectivity. Initial ticket logged the event as P3 pending review. Customer impact confirmed across an estimated 12k-40k subscribers depending on which count is current; NOC chat still disagrees on the number.

gold_answer.rationale: Full loss of upstream connectivity for an entire region with confirmed customer impact is a cascading outage regardless of the disputed subscriber count or the stale P3 ticket label.

task_input: Aggregation switch AGG-19 lost one of three parallel uplinks during a 03:00 maintenance window; the remaining two uplinks are carrying 340 Mbps of the normal 900 Mbps load at 55% utilization, and 1,200 subscribers are on this switch. No customer complaints logged.

gold_answer.rationale: Redundancy is reduced but two of three uplinks are still serving traffic well under capacity; degraded-but-serving, not an outage.

task_input: Cell site CS-410 lost all three sectors at 09:10 during a firmware push; service was fully restored by 09:22 after an automatic rollback. Current status: normal, no active alarms, all sectors reporting green.

gold_answer.rationale: The outage already happened and fully resolved twelve minutes later via automatic rollback; classify on current state, not the resolved historical event, so this is a follow-up review, not an active P1.

task_input: Core router CR-04 line card LC-3 overheated and forced a BGP session reset to route reflectors RR-01 and RR-02; this in turn caused downstream OLT-SOUTH-7 to log 340 spurious link-flap alarms and DNS resolver DNS-C2 to briefly report elevated latency as a secondary effect of the reroute.

Planned firmware upgrade on DWDM node DW-31 triggered an unexpected loss-of-signal alarm on span FS-204 between POP-NORTH and POP-EAST; 18 downstream circuits briefly flapped as a secondary symptom before the node's protection switch engaged.

On-call initially suspected baseband unit BBU-88 at cell site CS-512 after sector S2 went offline; further diagnostics ruled out BBU-88 and traced the fault to a failed fiber jumper between BBU-88 and the remote radio head RRH-2.

Earlier reports at 08:40 described voicemail platform VM-CLUSTER-B as fully down; that was later corrected — the platform stayed up throughout, and only new-mailbox provisioning was delayed 08:40-09:15 for about 200 accounts. Provisioning queue is now fully drained.

gold_answer.summary: New-mailbox provisioning on voicemail platform VM-CLUSTER-B was delayed from 08:40 to 09:15 for about 200 accounts; the platform itself remained available throughout despite an earlier incorrect full-outage report, and the provisioning queue has fully drained.
gold_answer.impact: About 200 new-mailbox provisioning requests were delayed 35 minutes; existing voicemail service was not affected.

task_input: SMS gateway SG-7 was degraded 11:00-11:40 due to a peer link certificate issue with partner P-TEL, matching last month's SG-3 incident pattern. Queue drained by 11:40 with zero message loss. No relation to today's separate DNS-C2 config push, which is unaffected and not part of this incident.

gold_answer.summary: SMS gateway SG-7 was degraded from 11:00 to 11:40 due to a peer-link certificate issue with partner P-TEL; the queue fully drained by 11:40 with no message loss.
gold_answer.impact: Outbound SMS to P-TEL was delayed up to 40 minutes; no messages were lost.

task_input: Edge router ER-44's nightly backup failed for the fourth time this week at 02:00; the device continues forwarding traffic normally and backup connectivity from three other devices on the same management VLAN is confirmed healthy. No customer-facing service is named anywhere in this report.

gold_answer.summary: Edge router ER-44's nightly configuration backup failed for the fourth consecutive night; the device is forwarding traffic normally and management-plane connectivity from peer devices is healthy.
gold_answer.impact: No customer or service impact; a recurring backup job failure needs investigation.

task_input: Core router CR-07 line card LC-1 failed and forced failover to CR-08; there is no redundancy remaining on this path. 22,000 subscribers are currently served entirely through CR-08 with no standby. A vendor ticket was opened last week for an unrelated issue on CR-07 and is still open.

gold_answer.action: Dispatch field engineering immediately to replace line card LC-1 on CR-07 and restore redundancy.
gold_answer.justification: 22,000 subscribers are running with no remaining redundancy on a single failover path; a second failure would cause a full regional outage, and this is unrelated to the open vendor ticket.

task_input: DNS resolver cluster DNS-C2 has been returning SERVFAIL for 31% of queries since last night's config push CP-2310; the error rate is climbing roughly 2 points per hour, and 9,000 subscribers are affected. A validated staged rollback is ready to run.

gold_answer.action: Roll back config push CP-2310 on DNS-C2 immediately using the validated staged rollback.
gold_answer.justification: A climbing 31% query failure rate affecting 9,000 subscribers is active, worsening customer impact with a ready and validated fix available; monitoring further would let the failure rate keep climbing.

task_input: Aggregation switch AGG-33 uplink flapped twice overnight but has been stable for six hours; redundant path held throughout with no service interruption. On-call's instinct is to just monitor, but flapping links have preceded full failures twice before on this switch model.

gold_answer.action: Schedule a proactive uplink inspection and replacement during the next maintenance window rather than only monitoring.
gold_answer.justification: The link is currently stable and redundancy held, so it is not immediate, but this switch model has a known history of flapping preceding full failure — passive monitoring alone would ignore that pattern.
